title: Desmodium stoloniferum
intro: Desmodium stoloniferum, also known as creeping tick trefoil, is a perennial legume native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Desmodium stoloniferum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Desmodium stoloniferum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Desmodium stoloniferum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. More specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and is part of the tribe Desmodieae. The genus Desmodium is native to Asia and North America, and D. stoloniferum is a native species to North America. It is a perennial herbaceous plant with a stoloniferous growth habit, meaning it produces long stems that root at the nodes. This unique growth habit allows D. stoloniferum to form dense colonies and spread quickly in its native habitats.
uses: Desmodium stoloniferum, also known as hempvine or creeping tickclover, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Its leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties. In some cultures, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is also used to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential side effects.

The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and digestive disorders.
